title: SSOT Registry — Claim graph document (interop issue #12)

vocab.$comment: Controlled vocab for the claims collection (object/domain reuse the declaration axes; status is claims-specific). Adopted via set_vocab; sjv enforces values (hard) in rules.py, cardinality is soft.

id.$comment: Opaque sjv-minted surrogate (UUID), permanent, never recomputed (Decision A applies to claims too). Match on claim_id, never on id.

claim_id.$comment: Stable human-readable handle (e.g. 'T-SNAP') — the greppable natural key, like a declaration's qualified. Referenced by declarations[*].claims.witness_of and by from/to.

object.$comment: Reuses the object vocab; multi-value (element-aware enum in rules.py).

domain.$comment: Reuses the domain vocab; multi-value; element-aware enum.

status.$comment: Config-driven vocab (built-in floor UNION adopted vocab, like role). Floor: commitment, conj, corr, deep, proved. The killer cross-collection invariant gates proved/deep on a live witness.

from.$comment: Populated => this claim is an EDGE; references another claim's claim_id.

to.$comment: Populated => this claim is an EDGE; references another claim's claim_id.

history.$comment: Append-only status provenance; downgrades KEPT, never erased (T5).

$comment: claim_id uniqueness, status/object/domain enum (floor UNION vocab), and from/to reference integrity are enforced in consumers/claims/rules.py. The cross-collection witness invariant lives at the store level (core/engine.Store cross_validators).
